response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior/staff software engineer with nearly two decades of full-stack, high-throughput backend, and open-source experience, but the role requires 8+ years shipping production-grade AI solutions and his AI exposure is limited to roughly one year of MCP tooling and agentic-workflow infrastructure work at re:cinq. There is no demonstrated experience with RAG architectures, vector databases, LLM evaluation pipelines, multi-agent frameworks, or Python — several of which are hard requirements — so despite genuine adjacent strengths he falls well short of the core GenAI qualifications for a staff forward-deployed role.
strengths: Current hands-on MCP server/code-generator and agentic-workflow tooling experience, directly relevant to the role's key technologies
18+ years of production engineering across TypeScript/Node.js, distributed low-latency systems, and cloud/Kubernetes deployment
Proven teaching, workshop, and consulting background suggesting strong customer-facing and mentoring capability
concerns: About 1 year of AI-related experience versus the required 8+ years building production AI solutions
No evidence of RAG, vector databases, LLM evaluation/observability pipelines, or multi-agent frameworks like LangGraph/CrewAI
Python absent from skills and cloud experience limited to AWS CDK for a pricing service, not AI systems on major cloud platforms

key_factors.factor: GenAI production experience gap (8+ years required)
Strong staff-level engineering breadth and current MCP/agentic tooling work
